content.content:
220	    /// Extracts a substring of the string.
221	    /// Fails with an error if the start or end index is out of bounds.
222	    #[func]
223	    pub fn slice(
224	        &self,
225	        /// The start byte index (inclusive). If negative, indexes from the
226	        /// back.
227	        start: i64,
228	        /// The end byte index (exclusive). If omitted, the whole slice until
229	        /// the end of the string is extracted. If negative, indexes from the
230	        /// back.
231	        #[default]
232	        end: Option<i64>,
233	        /// The number of bytes to extract. This is equivalent to passing
234	        /// `start + count` as the `end` position. Mutually exclusive with `end`.
235	        #[named]
236	        count: Option<i64>,
237	    ) -> StrResult<Str> {
238	        let end = end.or(count.map(|c| start + c)).unwrap_or(self.len() as i64);
239	        let start = self.locate(start)?;
240	        let end = self.locate(end)?.max(start);
241	        Ok(self.0[start..end].into())
242	    }
243	
244	    /// Returns the grapheme clusters of the string as an array of substrings.
245	    #[func]
246	    pub fn clusters(&self) -> Array {
247	        self.as_str().graphemes(true).map(|s| Value::Str(s.into())).collect()
248	    }
249	
250	    /// Returns the Unicode codepoints of the string as an array of substrings.
251	    #[func]
252	    pub fn codepoints(&self) -> Array {
253	        self.chars().map(|c| Value::Str(c.into())).collect()
254	    }

input.command:
ls -la /tmp/scratch/out.pdf
echo '#"123".first(pattern: "alpha")' > /tmp/scratch/err.typ
echo '#"".first()' > /tmp/scratch/empty.typ
echo '#"abc".first(pattern: "bogus")' > /tmp/scratch/badpat.typ
export PATH="/usr/local/cargo/bin:$PATH"
target/release/typst compile /tmp/scratch/err.typ /tmp/scratch/err.pdf 2>&1
echo "---"
target/release/typst compile /tmp/scratch/empty.typ /tmp/scratch/empty.pdf 2>&1
echo "---"
target/release/typst compile /tmp/scratch/badpat.typ /tmp/scratch/badpat.pdf 2>&1

content.text:
Both features are implemented in `crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs`:

- **`pattern` param** (`CharClass` enum: `alpha`, `numeric`, `alphanumeric`, `uppercase`, `lowercase`, `whitespace`) scans graphemes forward/backward for the first cluster whose leading char matches, erroring descriptively if none match or if an unknown string is passed (handled automatically by `derive(Cast)`).
- **`case` param** (`LetterCase` enum: `upper`, `lower`, `title`) transforms the returned grapheme, with title-case uppercasing the first scalar and lowercasing the rest.

Verified by building the `typst-cli` binary and running real `.typ` snippets — correct output for all the spec's examples, plus proper errors for empty strings, no-match patterns, and invalid pattern names. The diff is written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`.

result:
Both features are implemented in `crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs`:

- **`pattern` param** (`CharClass` enum: `alpha`, `numeric`, `alphanumeric`, `uppercase`, `lowercase`, `whitespace`) scans graphemes forward/backward for the first cluster whose leading char matches, erroring descriptively if none match or if an unknown string is passed (handled automatically by `derive(Cast)`).
- **`case` param** (`LetterCase` enum: `upper`, `lower`, `title`) transforms the returned grapheme, with title-case uppercasing the first scalar and lowercasing the rest.

Verified by building the `typst-cli` binary and running real `.typ` snippets — correct output for all the spec's examples, plus proper errors for empty strings, no-match patterns, and invalid pattern names. The diff is written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`.
